meta.description: UX evaluation criteria for assessing the user experience quality of generated MSM Portal screens. Used in Phase 5 of the generation protocol.

information_hierarchy.name: Clear page title and context
information_hierarchy.question: Does the page have a clear title that tells the user where they are and what they can do?
check_for: MCContentLayout with title prop (list-page pattern)
MCFormLayout with breadCrumbs prop (create-page, edit-page patterns)
Breadcrumb navigation reflecting current path
information_hierarchy.pass: Page has a descriptive title via MCContentLayout.title or MCFormLayout.breadCrumbs, and breadcrumbs show navigation context
information_hierarchy.fail_example: Page renders content without any title, header, or navigation context

information_hierarchy.name: Visual hierarchy matches importance
information_hierarchy.question: Are primary actions visually prominent? Are secondary elements subdued?
check_for: Primary action uses MCButton2 variant='contained'
Only ONE primary (contained) action per view
Secondary actions use default or text variant
MCContentLayout.rightAccessory for page-level primary action (e.g., Create button)
information_hierarchy.pass: One contained button for primary action, other buttons are default/text variant. Create button in MCContentLayout.rightAccessory on list pages.
information_hierarchy.fail_example: Multiple contained buttons competing for attention, or primary action buried in secondary styling

information_hierarchy.name: Logical content grouping
information_hierarchy.question: Is related content grouped together with clear visual boundaries?
check_for: MCFormPanel with MCFormPanelTitle for form sections
MCFormFieldGroup for related fields within a panel
MCBarTabs for content categories (tab-navigation pattern)
MCFormPanelBody wrapping field groups
information_hierarchy.pass: Related fields are grouped in MCFormPanel with descriptive MCFormPanelTitle. MCFormFieldGroup groups related inputs.
information_hierarchy.fail_example: All form fields in one flat list with no MCFormPanel grouping

information_hierarchy.name: Progressive disclosure
information_hierarchy.question: Is complex information revealed progressively rather than overwhelming the user?
check_for: MCBarTabs for content categories on detail/list pages
MCFormPanel sections for logical form grouping
Expandable sections for advanced options
Sub-containers per tab for lazy data loading (list-page pattern)
information_hierarchy.pass: Secondary/advanced options are in separate MCFormPanel sections or tabs. List page uses tab-based sub-containers for different entity states.
information_hierarchy.fail_example: Every field and option visible at once on a create form with no panel grouping

user_flow.name: Clear entry and exit points
user_flow.question: Can the user clearly see how to start the task and how to leave/cancel?
check_for: MCFormActions with Cancel + Submit buttons (form-basic pattern)
MCFormLayout.onClose for form page exit (form-full-page pattern)
MCFormLayout.footerContent with Cancel and Submit
Breadcrumb navigation for back-tracking
user_flow.pass: Form has Cancel + Submit in MCFormActions. Full-page forms have MCFormLayout.onClose. Detail/edit pages have breadcrumb navigation.
user_flow.fail_example: Create form with only a Submit button and no Cancel or onClose handler

user_flow.name: Success feedback
user_flow.question: Does the user receive clear feedback after completing an action?
check_for: fireSuccess() toast from useInAppAlert after successful mutation
Navigation to appropriate page: create → list page, edit → detail page
Query invalidation or refetch after mutation success
user_flow.pass: After submit: fireSuccess(t('message.create.success')) → navigate to list/detail page. Create navigates to list (MERouteKey.{ENTITY}_MAIN), edit navigates to detail (MERouteKey.{ENTITY}_DETAIL).
user_flow.fail_example: Form submits but user sees no toast confirmation and stays on the same page

user_flow.name: Error recovery
user_flow.question: Can the user understand and recover from errors?
check_for: Field-level errors via Formik/Yup validation (errors shown after meta.touched)
API error displayed via fireCollapsibleError({ summary, error })
Query errors trigger fireCollapsibleError + navigate away (error-handling pattern)
Mutation errors show collapsible error but stay on page
user_flow.pass: Yup validation errors appear next to the field after blur. API errors show in collapsible alert via fireCollapsibleError with summary string.
user_flow.fail_example: Generic error message with no field-level indication, or error silently logged to console

user_flow.name: Destructive action confirmation
user_flow.question: Are destructive actions (delete, remove, disable) protected by a confirmation step?
check_for: MCCommonDialog for delete confirmation (delete-confirm-dialog pattern)
Dialog title and message explain the consequence using i18n
Dialog has Cancel button and destructive action button
Entity name included in confirmation message via t() interpolation
user_flow.pass: Delete triggers MCCommonDialog explaining what will be lost. Dialog includes entity name: t('message.deleteConfirm', { name: entity.name }). Cancel is available.
user_flow.fail_example: Delete button immediately removes the entity without MCCommonDialog confirmation

user_flow.name: Consistent navigation patterns
user_flow.question: Does the user flow follow the standard MSM Portal navigation patterns?
check_for: List page → click row → Detail page (via generatePathForRoute with MERouteKey.{ENTITY}_DETAIL)
Detail page → Edit button → Edit form (MERouteKey.{ENTITY}_EDIT)
Create button → Full-page form with MCFormLayout (MERouteKey.{ENTITY}_CREATE)
Breadcrumb reflects current path via MCFormLayout.breadCrumbs or MCContentLayout.showBreadcrumb
Navigation uses generatePathForRoute + useEntityParam for type-safe route generation
user_flow.pass: Navigation follows List → Detail → Edit flow using generatePathForRoute with correct MERouteKey values
user_flow.fail_example: Edit opens in a dialog instead of full-page MCFormLayout, or navigates using hardcoded paths instead of generatePathForRoute

interaction_quality.name: Responsive to user input
interaction_quality.question: Does the UI respond immediately to user actions?
check_for: Submit button disabled={isSubmitting} during mutation (form-basic pattern)
MCCircularLoader fillParent during data fetch (loading-state pattern)
MCLoader for inline loading states
interaction_quality.pass: Submit button shows disabled state during mutation via disabled={isSubmitting}. Data fetching shows MCCircularLoader. No unresponsive gaps.
interaction_quality.fail_example: User clicks Submit, button remains enabled and nothing happens visually until API responds

interaction_quality.name: Form validation timing
interaction_quality.question: Is validation triggered at the right moment?
check_for: Errors shown after field blur (meta.touched via Formik)
MCForm* components show errors only when showError is true (default) and field is touched
Submit triggers validation of all fields via Formik validateOnSubmit
state-machines.json → formInputs base machine: idle → touched → error only after touch
interaction_quality.pass: Errors appear after user leaves a field (onBlur per Formik). Untouched fields show no error. Submit validates all.
interaction_quality.fail_example: Error messages appear immediately when form loads, before user interacts with any field

interaction_quality.name: Consistent action placement
interaction_quality.question: Are action buttons consistently positioned?
check_for: MCFormActions at bottom of form with Cancel left and Submit right
MCFormLayout.footerContent for full-page form actions
MCContentLayout.rightAccessory for page-level actions (Create button on list pages)
MCMoreActionsButton for table row-level actions (action-button pattern)
interaction_quality.pass: Cancel left, Submit right in MCFormActions. Page-level primary action in MCContentLayout.rightAccessory. Row actions via MCMoreActionsButton in last column.
interaction_quality.fail_example: Submit button at the top of the form, or primary action on the left side of MCFormActions

ux_writing.name: Action labels are specific
ux_writing.question: Do buttons and calls to action describe the actual outcome instead of generic UI verbs?
check_for: Primary actions use outcome-based labels such as 'Save changes', 'Submit order', or '비밀번호 재설정 이메일 보내기'
Generic labels like 'Submit', 'Confirm', or '보내기' are avoided unless the exact domain meaning is obvious
ux_writing.pass: Action labels tell the user what will happen after clicking.
ux_writing.fail_example: A context-specific action is labeled only as 'Submit' or '보내기'.

ux_writing.name: Error and validation copy supports recovery
ux_writing.question: When something goes wrong, does the copy explain the next step?
check_for: Field errors explain what to fix
Toast and alert copy tells the user what to retry, review, or update
Messages avoid blameful phrasing
ux_writing.pass: The message names the problem and includes the next helpful action when possible.
ux_writing.fail_example: The UI only says 'Error occurred.' or '잘못 입력했습니다.'

ux_writing.name: Terminology is consistent across the screen
ux_writing.question: Is the same concept named the same way in labels, dialogs, empty states, and toasts?
check_for: Domain nouns such as draft, review, workplace, and order use one stable term
Labels, tabs, and status chips use the same terminology family
ux_writing.pass: The screen uses one consistent term for each domain concept.
ux_writing.fail_example: The same concept is called 'draft', 'temporary save', and 'saved version' on one flow.

ux_writing.name: Empty states and confirmations are informative
ux_writing.question: Do empty states and dialogs explain the situation and the next decision clearly?
check_for: Empty states explain what is missing and how to proceed
Confirmation dialogs explain consequences and name the action clearly
ux_writing.pass: The user understands both the current state and the next safe action.
ux_writing.fail_example: An empty state says only 'No data.' or a confirmation dialog uses only '확인'.

accessibility.name: Form labels present
accessibility.question: Does every form input have a visible label?
check_for: fieldLabel prop on all MCForm* components
Labels describe the field clearly using i18n: fieldLabel={t('field.name')}
required prop set correctly (required={false} shows '(Optional)' suffix per conventions.json → formComponentRules)
accessibility.pass: Every MCForm* input has fieldLabel prop set with a translated string. required prop accurately reflects field requirement.
accessibility.fail_example: MCFormTextInput with no fieldLabel, relying only on placeholder text

accessibility.name: Keyboard navigable
accessibility.question: Can the user complete the task using only a keyboard?
check_for: All interactive elements are focusable (Tab order follows visual order)
MCCommonDialog traps focus per patterns.json → accessibility
No click-only interactions without keyboard equivalent
MCFormTextInput, MCButton2, MCBarTabs all support keyboard interaction per components.json → accessibility
accessibility.pass: All interactive elements are focusable and operable via keyboard. Dialogs trap focus. Tab order is logical.
accessibility.fail_example: Custom dropdown that only opens on mouse click with no keyboard handler

accessibility.name: Error messages linked to fields
accessibility.question: Are error messages programmatically associated with their form fields?
check_for: MCFormFieldError linked via aria-describedby (automatic in MCForm* components per components.json → accessibility)
Screen reader announces error inline when field is focused
Error messages use role='alert' for immediate announcement
accessibility.pass: MCForm* components automatically link errors via aria-describedby. Screen readers announce errors when field is focused.
accessibility.fail_example: Error message rendered as plain text with no programmatic association to the input

completeness.name: All required views exist
completeness.question: For the requested scope: are all expected views present?
check_for: List page with MCContentLayout, MCBarTabs, and Create button (list-page pattern)
Detail page with read-only MCFormPanel display (detail-page pattern)
Create form with MCFormLayout and Formik (create-page pattern)
Edit form reusing Create form with mode='edit' (edit-page pattern)
Delete confirmation dialog (delete-confirm-dialog pattern)
completeness.pass: All views requested by the user are present with correct patterns applied. Edit reuses Create form component.
completeness.fail_example: List and Create pages exist but no Detail or Edit pages when full CRUD was requested

completeness.name: Empty state messaging
completeness.question: Does the UI handle empty data gracefully?
check_for: Empty table shows descriptive message (not blank space)
Empty detail shows appropriate fallback
Create button/action visible from empty state
completeness.pass: Empty list shows message with call-to-action to create first entity. Empty states use i18n strings.
completeness.fail_example: Empty table shows blank white space with no explanation or guidance

completeness.name: Loading skeleton appropriate
completeness.question: Is the loading state proportional to expected wait?
check_for: MCCircularLoader fillParent for full-page initial data load (Container level)
MCLoader for partial/inline refreshes
Conditional render pattern: isFetching || !data ? <MCCircularLoader fillParent /> : <Component />
completeness.pass: Full-page MCCircularLoader for initial page data. Inline MCLoader for partial refreshes. Loading state matches scope per patterns.json → loading-state.
completeness.fail_example: Entire page shows spinner when only one panel's data is refreshing

completeness.name: Route registration complete
completeness.question: Are all new pages properly registered in the routing system?
check_for: MERouteKey enum value added (route-registration pattern step 1)
Route template entry with path and breadcrumb handle (step 2)
Client route mapping with allowedRoles (step 3)
completeness.pass: All 3 steps of route-registration pattern completed for every new page
completeness.fail_example: Page component exists but is not reachable because route was not registered

scoring.method: Each criterion is scored as: pass (2), partial (1), fail (0). Score is weighted by importance.
scoring.max_possible: Sum of (2 * weight) for all applicable criteria

thresholds.good: 75-89% weighted score, no more than 1 high-weight fail

evaluation_template.description: Template for reporting UX evaluation results. Agents should fill this structure after evaluating generated code.

criteria_results.evidence: string (what was observed in the generated code)
criteria_results.recommendation: string (specific fix if not pass, referencing design system component or pattern)

top_3_improvements: string (most impactful improvement, referencing specific component/pattern)
